description: Add first-class function definitions and lexical closures to ArithLang using fn(params) => expr and fn(params) { block } syntax, with immutable-by-default variable capture and explicit mutable capture via mut(var) clauses.

userStories.title: Tokenize function keywords and operators
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the lexer to recognize `fn`, `return`, and `=>` so the parser can handle function syntax.
acceptanceCriteria: `fn` tokenized as TOK_FN in the TokenType enum and lexer
`return` tokenized as TOK_RETURN in the TokenType enum and lexer
`=>` tokenized as TOK_ARROW in the TokenType enum and lexer
Existing tokens and keywords are unaffected
Build passes (cmake --build build)
Unit tests pass (cd build && ctest)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define AST nodes for functions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need AST node classes for function literals, function calls, and return statements so the compiler pipeline can represent functions structurally.
acceptanceCriteria: Supporting structs defined: FunctionParameter (name, is_mutable, location), CapturedVariable (name, is_mutable_capture, location), ClosureContext (immutable/mutable capture maps), FunctionValue (llvm_function, closure_context)
FunctionLiteralAST class defined in include/function_ast.h with: parameter list, capture list, body (ASTNode*), is_expression_function flag, source location
FunctionCallAST class defined with: callee ExprAST, argument list, source location
ReturnStmtAST class defined with: optional return value ExprAST, source location
All new AST classes inherit from ExprAST or ASTNode as appropriate and declare codegen() override
Build passes (cmake --build build)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Parse expression-style function literals
userStories.description: As a user, I want to write `add = fn(x, y) => x + y;` so I can define simple single-expression functions.
acceptanceCriteria: parseFunctionLiteral() added to parser; dispatched from parsePrimary() on TOK_FN
`fn(params) => expr` parsed into FunctionLiteralAST with is_expression_function = true
Parameters parsed as a comma-separated list of identifiers inside ()
Trailing comma in parameter list produces a parse error with source location
Unit tests in tests/test_function_parser.cpp cover basic expression function parsing
Build passes (cmake --build build)
Unit tests pass (cd build && ctest)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Parse block-style function literals
userStories.description: As a user, I want to write `fn(x) { result = x * 2; return result; }` so I can define multi-statement functions.
acceptanceCriteria: `fn(params) { statements }` parsed into FunctionLiteralAST with is_expression_function = false
`return expr;` inside a block body parsed as ReturnStmtAST
parseReturnStatement() added to parser; dispatched from parseStatement() on TOK_RETURN
`return` at top level (outside any function body) is a parse error with source location
Unit tests in tests/test_function_parser.cpp cover block function and return statement parsing
Build passes (cmake --build build)
Unit tests pass (cd build && ctest)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Parse mutable parameter declarations
userStories.description: As a user, I want to write `fn(mut x, y) => x + y` so parameters can be declared mutable and reassigned inside the function body.
acceptanceCriteria: `mut` before a parameter name sets FunctionParameter::is_mutable = true
Parameters without `mut` prefix have is_mutable = false
Unit tests cover: all-immutable params, all-mutable params, mixed params
Build passes (cmake --build build)
Unit tests pass (cd build && ctest)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Parse capture clause for closures
userStories.description: As a user, I want to write `fn() mut(counter) { counter = counter + 1; return counter; }` so I can explicitly capture outer mutable variables.
acceptanceCriteria: parseCaptureClause() added to parser; recognized as `mut(var1, var2)` after the parameter list and before the body
Parsed capture names stored as CapturedVariable entries in FunctionLiteralAST
Absence of capture clause results in an empty capture list (not an error)
Unit tests cover: no capture clause, single capture, multiple captures
Build passes (cmake --build build)
Unit tests pass (cd build && ctest)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Parse function call expressions
userStories.description: As a user, I want to write `add(1, 2)` to call a function stored in a variable.
acceptanceCriteria: parsePostfixExpr() extended to detect `(` after any primary expression and produce FunctionCallAST
parseFunctionCall() parses comma-separated argument list and closing `)`
Zero-argument calls `f()` are valid
Unit tests in tests/test_function_parser.cpp cover: 0-arg call, 1-arg call, multi-arg call, nested call `f(g(x))`
Build passes (cmake --build build)
Unit tests pass (cd build && ctest)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Type-check functions and calls
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the type checker to validate function definitions and call sites so users get clear errors before codegen.
acceptanceCriteria: validateFunctionLiteral() checks: no duplicate parameter names
validateCapturedVariables() checks: each captured variable exists in outer scope and was declared mutable; capturing an undeclared or immutable variable is an error with source location
validateFunctionCall() checks: argument count matches parameter count; emits error with source location on mismatch
validateReturnStatement() checks: `return` is inside a function body
Reassigning an immutable parameter inside a function body is a type-check error
All errors use existing GCC-style ParseError with SourceLocation
Unit tests in tests/test_function_parser.cpp cover each error case
Build passes (cmake --build build)
Unit tests pass (cd build && ctest)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Generate LLVM IR for expression functions
userStories.description: As a user, I want `add = fn(x, y) => x + y; print add(3, 4);` to compile and print `7.000000000000000`.
acceptanceCriteria: generateFunctionLiteral() in src/function_codegen.cpp emits a named llvm::Function for expression-body functions
Function parameters mapped to llvm::Argument values accessible within the body
generateFunctionCall() in src/codegen.cpp emits a llvm::CallInst
Integration test file tests/k/function_basic.k created: `add = fn(x, y) => x + y; result = add(20, 22); print result;` with `// EXPECTED: 42.000000000000000`
tests/k/function_basic.k passes via ./test_runner.sh
Build passes (cmake --build build)
Unit tests pass (cd build && ctest)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Generate LLVM IR for block functions with return
userStories.description: As a user, I want block-style functions with explicit `return` to compile and execute correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: generateFunctionLiteral() handles is_expression_function = false by generating a block body
generateReturnStatement() emits llvm::ReturnInst at the return statement
Expression functions use the expression value as the implicit return value
Integration test tests/k/function_block.k created with a block-style function using explicit return, passes via ./test_runner.sh
Build passes (cmake --build build)
Unit tests pass (cd build && ctest)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement immutable closures
userStories.description: As a user, I want `scale = fn(x) => x * multiplier;` to capture `multiplier` from the outer scope by value at definition time.
acceptanceCriteria: Variables referenced inside a function body that are not parameters and not in the mut() capture clause are automatically captured by value (double copy) at function definition time
Captured immutable values are embedded in a closure context struct passed as an implicit extra argument to the LLVM function
Subsequent changes to the outer variable do not affect the closure's captured value

Integration test tests/k/function_closure.k created: `multiplier = 10; scale = fn(x) => x * multiplier; result = scale(5); print result;` with `// EXPECTED: 50.000000000000000`, passes via ./test_runner.sh
Build passes (cmake --build build)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement mutable closures
userStories.description: As a user, I want `increment = fn() mut(counter) { counter = counter + 1; return counter; };` to modify and persist the outer `counter` across calls.
acceptanceCriteria: Mutable captures use std::shared_ptr<double> so closure and outer scope share the same heap allocation
Closure context struct stores shared_ptr for mutable captures, double for immutable captures
Multiple calls to the closure reflect accumulated mutable state
Integration test tests/k/function_mutable_capture.k created with expected output: `// EXPECTED: 1.000000000000000` and `// EXPECTED: 2.000000000000000`, passes via ./test_runner.sh
Build passes (cmake --build build)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Support higher-order functions
userStories.description: As a user, I want to pass functions as arguments and call them inside other functions, e.g. `apply(square, 5)` returns 25.
acceptanceCriteria: Function values can be passed as arguments to other functions and stored in variables
generateFunctionCall() correctly handles a callee that is itself a function call result or a parameter holding a function value

Integration test tests/k/function_higher_order.k created: `apply = fn(f, x) => f(x); square = fn(x) => x * x; result = apply(square, 5); print result;` with `// EXPECTED: 25.000000000000000`, passes via ./test_runner.sh
Build passes (cmake --build build)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Support recursive functions
userStories.description: As a user, I want to write a recursive function that calls itself by name, e.g. recursive factorial.
acceptanceCriteria: A function stored in immutable variable `f` can call `f(...)` inside its own body (self-reference via the variable binding)
Integration test tests/k/function_recursive.k created implementing recursive factorial of 5, with `// EXPECTED: 120.000000000000000`, passes via ./test_runner.sh
Build passes (cmake --build build)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update syntax specification and README
userStories.description: As a developer reading the docs, I need the language syntax spec and README to reflect the new function grammar.
acceptanceCriteria: specs/syntax.md BNF grammar updated to include <function_literal>, <call_expr>, <return_stmt>, <capture_clause>, and <parameter_list> rules
specs/syntax.md includes examples of: expression function, block function, closure with immutable capture, closure with mutable capture, higher-order function call
README.md features list updated to include: first-class function definitions (fn), lexical closures, mutable capture with mut(), higher-order functions, recursion
Build passes (cmake --build build)
Typecheck passes
